name: CSBP
subtitle: Codex Shared Best Practice
description: A lightweight three-file protocol kit for keeping recurring Codex best practices explicit, reviewed, and separate from automatic memory or AGENTS.md.
purpose: Keep recurring Codex best practices explicit, reviewed, operator-approved, and separate from automatic memory or host contract bloat.
authority_order: local project instructions or project-local guidance

files.role: Bootstrap and routing layer. Defines system identity, authority order, usage modes, read order, and write restrictions.
Runtime layer. Stores promoted practices only. Active practices may guide work when relevant.
files.mutable_at_runtime: Only through compiler path and operator approval.

files.role: Formation layer. Evaluates, normalizes, promotes, revises, deprecates, removes, or rejects candidate practices.
files.read_timing: Only when the operator opens practice formation or maintenance work.

runtime_behavior: No practice changes without operator approval.
Applies already-promoted active practices only when applies_when matches current work.
No active match means no forced behavior.
formation_path: Operator raises or asks for a candidate.
Codex evaluates recurrence, scope, actionability, and authority.
The candidate is normalized into runtime block shape.
Weak or local material is rejected.
practice_admission_criteria: expressible as a short operational instruction
not already covered by stronger local instruction
likely to reduce recurring waste or error
rejection_criteria: too narrow to matter outside one case
dependent on hidden or unstable context
compatibility: If CSBP is the persistent best-practice layer, avoid running it alongside automatic memory systems that can also turn session carryover into future behavior.
